v0_to_v1_prompt_delta: v0 prompt run scored 1.000 on v0 (0.905 on v1 strict-item re-judge — the lowest of the four books on v1, primarily missing economics-math and counter-prescription). v1 prompt run scores 1.000 on v1 — the prompt update fully addressed both gaps with explicit CAC payback calculation and explicit counter-condition.

item_scores.reasoning: Two-candidate ICP analysis: primary tier-1 enterprise (firmographic + behavioral + multiple buyer roles) + secondary AI-native fintech (deferred from focus). Named accounts listed (Itaú, Bradesco, Banco do Brasil, Nubank, Magalu, Via, Vivo, TIM, Claro). Named buyer roles (Head of AI / CDO / Director of AI Engineering as economic buyer; CISO + DPO as gatekeepers; CTO as champion-or-blocker).

Comprehensive role audit: SDR absent in Brazil, AE absent, AM/CSM absent, founder/exec sales implicit (US-based execs flying in for Brazilian deals). Net diagnosis: 'Brazil is a zero-headcount territory served by partner channel and self-serve.' Names structural risk of generalist Country Manager hire.

All sources named with stage-relative diagnosis: Seeds weak (no flagship references), Nets very weak (no Portuguese content, minimal events, USD pricing causing FX abandonment), Spears effectively zero. Plus channel category added: AWS Bedrock dominates by default but uncontrolled — 'Bedrock customers can use Claude without Anthropic ever knowing who they are.' Sophisticated framing.

Hybrid path: declines aggressive Cold Calling 2.0 for Q2 with 4 explicit when-NOT-to-apply-Spears conditions (ICP not validated by closed-won, Nets not yet tried, PLG/channel cheaply producing buyers, compliance-gated procurement is the actual blocker). Prescribes narrow trigger-driven outbound for Q3 with all specifics: buyer role (Director of AI / CDO), channel (LinkedIn InMail Portuguese + email follow with referral-pattern), trigger events, message shape (LGPD posture + Brazilian residency + named peer reference), expected reply rate 8-12% on referral-pattern with trigger.

5 specific ratios with target ranges: named-account list to meetings booked (15-20% trigger / 3-5% cold), meetings to qualified opps (40%), qualified opps to POC (50%), pilot to closed-won (30-40% in regulated), Bedrock-sourced spend to direct-contract conversion (>50% of >$25K/month accounts within 2 quarters). All with Brazilian-context calibration.

6 modes flagged with cited input evidence: founder-led sales without specialization (US execs flying in), confusing channel revenue for direct demand (Bedrock as uncontrolled lead source), would-be ICP-skipping (prescribed against), ignoring compliance as procurement gate, language/positioning mismatch (Portuguese defaults to 'ChatGPT'), pricing friction (USD FX exposure).

4 quarters with explicit dependencies: Q2 foundation (4 numbered steps with named dependencies), Q3 Nets + narrow Spears (3 steps depending on Q2 work + flagship reference), Q4 scale conditional on Q3 results, Q4 BRL invoicing depends on finance/legal sign-off. Plus public-sector / SOE explicitly deferred to separate Q4+ motion.

ICP 7/10, Outbound 8/10 — both as integers with rationale. 3 specific open questions with named-trigger language: actual Bedrock-sourced Brazilian revenue and account list, whether any tier-1 bank has signed multi-year direct Claude contract (binary trigger), Anthropic global capacity to staff Brazil GM (binary trigger).

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Two observable triggers named: (1) 'public disclosure of multi-vendor LLM pilot transitioning to single-vendor RFP' with named detection sources (Valor Econômico, Brazil Journal, Neofeed, LinkedIn job postings) OR ANPD enforcement action against peer institution (Diário Oficial), within 90-day window. (2) Secondary: monthly Bedrock Claude spend crossing $25K/month from single Brazilian-billed account (observable via Bedrock partner data). Both are detectable from outside the prospect company with named sources.

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Full CAC payback calculation: AE fully loaded $280-350K/year (with named cost components — salary, benefits, Brazilian payroll tax via CLT/PJ, travel, tooling), SDR $90-120K/year, combined sales cost $400-470K/year. Brazilian regulated-buyer ACV range $250K-$1M+, blended $400K. Math: AE+SDR pair needs ~3 net-new closes/year for 12-month CAC payback at 3:1 LTV:CAC, 90% margin, 2-year contract life. Sets floor quota: 'if AE cannot land 3 enterprise contracts in year one, unit economics fail and motion should be re-scoped to channel-only.'

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Two named counter-conditions with specific thresholds: (1) 'If by end of Q2 2026, monthly Bedrock-sourced Claude spend from Brazilian-billed accounts has not grown >40% quarter-over-quarter, the channel-led thesis is failing and outbound must be pulled forward to Q2 regardless of compliance and content readiness.' (2) Inverse: 'If a flagship bank signs a direct contract in Q2 via founder-led sales, delay SDR hiring another quarter and double down on Seeds before Spears.' Both observable triggers, both name what would force a different action.
